behavior_summary: This script implements the runtime behavior of a 'water spell' process: when the spell process executes it checks the process's target result and either spawns a standalone/free effect or makes a targeted item be hurled away and then triggers a follow-up handler.
If the process target query returns zero (no valid item/target), the routine spawns a FREE effect (FREE::29D8) with fixed constants and returns — acting as a cleanup/visual fallback.

If there is a target, it calls Item::getDirToItem to compute a cardinal/octile direction toward the target and to obtain an item/address out-parameter, maps that direction to hard-coded X/Y delta bytes (hurl velocities), calls Item::hurl to throw the item with those deltas, then calls a follow-up METHOD::0080 with the direction and the item/out parameter to handle animation/state chaining.
notable_control_flow: Entry as a process handler: implicit pid is used to call process target().
Immediate branch on process target() == 0: spawn FREE::29D8(0x64,0x7C,0) and return (guard/cleanup path).
Otherwise: call Item::getDirToItem(1, target, &out) to obtain a direction index (0..7) and an out/address; store direction in BP-08 and item/out in BP-02.
Direction-driven switch: translate direction value into a pair of hard-coded byte deltas assigned to BP-04 (x) and BP-06 (y).
Invoke Item::hurl(pid, 0x02, 0x0C, y-delta, x-delta, &BP-02) to launch the target; then call METHOD::0080(0x01, direction, 0x01, &BP-02) for follow-up.
Several calls are wrapped with the engine's implies/pop/suspend idiom (suspension points) — inferred to allow asynchronous action or cooperative scheduling during the process.
open_questions: What exact visual/side-effect behavior does FREE::29D8 produce (visual splash, damage, or a simple cleanup)? — appears to be an effect but exact semantics are not certain.
Does BP-02 hold an item instance pointer or an item identifier/index after Item::getDirToItem and/or Item::hurl (the code treats it as an address/out param but exact type is inferred)?
What internal behavior does METHOD::0080 (called at 057C:U8P_WATRSPEL::0080) perform after hurling — animation, state change, linking to other spell effects, or queued processing?
Are the hard-coded delta bytes strictly velocity components in engine coordinates, and how do negative/overflow values map to actual direction/physics in the runtime?

main_function.name: U8P_WATRSPEL::0080
main_function.purpose: Implement the run-time effect of the water spell when the process executes: determine whether there is a valid target; if none, spawn a free effect; if present, compute direction and throw the target item and then trigger a follow-up handler.
entry_conditions: Invoked when the water-spell process runs (implicit pid of the process is used).
Triggered by the spell's runtime processing (likely on impact or area affect).

function_map.name: U8P_WATRSPEL::0080
function_map.role: Process handler for the water-spell effect; decides spawn vs. hurl and orchestrates follow-up.

function_map.name: U8P_WATRSPEL::process target
function_map.role: Query current process's target/result (used to decide spawn vs operate on item).

function_map.name: FREE::29D8
function_map.role: Spawn a free/standalone effect (called on no-target path); likely a visual/sound effect or cleanup.

function_map.name: Item::getDirToItem
function_map.role: Compute the direction (0..7) toward a target item and provide an out/address value used for subsequent actions.

function_map.name: Item::hurl
function_map.role: Cause the specified item to be hurled/thrown with given x/y deltas and flags; updates the item/out param.

function_map.name: METHOD::0080
function_map.role: Follow-up handler invoked after hurl to manage further state/animation/processing of the item or effect.

external_calls.purpose: Determine the current spell/process target; its zero/non-zero result controls the primary branch (spawn vs operate on item).
Spawn a free effect with constants (0x64, 0x7C, 0) when there is no valid target — acts as visual/cleanup fallback.
Compute direction index (0..7) toward the target and fill an out/address parameter (used for hurl).
Throw/hurl the identified item using computed x/y deltas and flags to create the physical displacement/animation.
Trigger post-hurl handling/processing for the item (likely animation/state update or chaining additional effects).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements the runtime behavior of a 'water spell' process: when the spell process executes it checks the process's target result and either spawns a standalone/free effect or makes a targeted item be hurled away and then triggers a follow-up handler.
